content:
Not long ago I found a patch about "Rust null block driver". The link to the patch lore is here
I also saw that these patches are applied in the rust-next branch, but I don't really know what are these branches about. Where could I find information about the different branches of the project?
Oh, the info wasn't that difficult to find after all. Thank you!

Does "the branch is now effectively frozen" mean that no new funtionality is intended to be added for the time being? Is the new code being added at the moment mostly related to bug fixes or is there more utility planned to be included for rust?

Does "the branch is now effectively frozen" mean that no new funtionality is intended to be added for the time being?

Yes, but not just for the time being, i.e. also forever. In other words, it is just used as a reference.

Is the new code being added at the moment mostly related to bug fixes or is there more utility planned to be included for rust?

New code goes to the mainline kernel (i.e. Linus').
new features are added on top of rust-next, as you observed with the "Rust null block driver" patchset

Alphyto said:

Not long ago I found a patch about "Rust null block driver". The link to the patch lore is here
I also saw that these patches are applied in the rust-next branch, but I don't really know what are these branches about. Where could I find information about the different branches of the project?

Let me know if you have any questions about the null block work, I will be happy to help :)

Miguel Ojeda ha dicho:

Yes, but not just for the time being, i.e. also forever. In other words, it is just used as a reference.

Okay, I was only careful to not assume anything too quickly

Andreas Hindborg ha dicho:

Let me know if you have any questions about the null block work, I will be happy to help :smile:

Then I will DM you for some doubts :grinning:

Kwanghoon Son 말함:

what is rust-dev branch for?
